code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent sentence.
    """
    # Simple mapping from predicate to a readable phrase.
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "buildingStartDate": "started construction in",
        "completionDate": "was completed in",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "location": "is located in",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "was designed by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "height": "has a height of",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is in",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "is led by",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "has postal code",
    }

    # Predicates where the natural English phrasing puts the object first.
    invert_map = {
        "leader": "is the leader of",
        "keyPerson": "is the key person at",
        "chancellor": "is the chancellor of",
        "capital": "is the capital of",
        "currency": "uses the currency of",
    }

    def clean_obj(obj: str) -> str:
        """Strip surrounding quotes and whitespace."""
        o = obj.strip()
        if (o.startswith('"') and o.endswith('"')) or (o.startswith("'") and o.endswith("'")):
            o = o[1:-1]
        return o

    clauses = []
    for triple in triples:
        obj = clean_obj(triple.object)
        # Use inverted phrasing when appropriate
        if triple.predicate in invert_map:
            clause = f"{obj} {invert_map[triple.predicate]} {triple.subject}"
        else:
            verb_phrase = verb_map.get(triple.predicate, "has")
            # Add plural handling for count‑type predicates
            if triple.predicate in {"floorCount", "bedCount", "numberOfRooms"}:
                clause = f"{triple.subject} {verb_phrase} {obj} floors"
            else:
                clause = f"{triple.subject} {verb_phrase} {obj}"
        clauses.append(clause.rstrip())

    # Join clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if not clauses:
        return ""
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its score and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Building. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: architecturalStyle - Example triple: (103 Colmore Row | architecturalStyle | Brutalist architecture)
Predicate: buildingStartDate - Example triple: (103 Colmore Row | buildingStartDate | "1973")
Predicate: completionDate - Example triple: (103 Colmore Row | completionDate | 1976)
Predicate: floorCount - Example triple: (103 Colmore Row | floorCount | 23)

Predicate: architect - Example triple: (11 Diagonal Street | architect | Helmut Jahn)
Predicate: owner - Example triple: (11 Diagonal Street | owner | Redefine Properties Limited)
Predicate: formerName - Example triple: (320 South Boston Building | formerName | "Exchange National Bank Building")
Predicate: height - Example triple: (320 South Boston Building | height | 121.92 (metres))
Predicate: buildingType - Example triple: (3Arena | buildingType | "Concert and events venue")
Predicate: developer - Example triple: (AC Hotel Bella Sky Copenhagen | developer | Bella Center)
Predicate: tenant - Example triple: (AC Hotel Bella Sky Copenhagen | tenant | Marriott International)
Predicate: isPartOf - Example triple: (Adare | isPartOf | Munster)
Predicate: country - Example triple: (Addis Ababa | country | Ethiopia)
Predicate: currentTenants - Example triple: (Addis Ababa City Hall | currentTenants | "Government of Addis Ababa")
Predicate: address - Example triple: (Adisham Hall | address | "St. Benedict's Monastery, Adisham, Haputhale, Sri Lanka")
Predicate: inaugurationDate - Example triple: (Alan B. Miller Hall | inaugurationDate | "2009-10-02")
